sarga.title: Rama advises Bharata to return back and rule the kingdom.

shlokas.original:
पुनरेवं ब्रुवाणं तं भरतं लक्ष्मणाग्रजः।
प्रत्युवाच तत श्श्रीमान् ज्ञातिमध्येऽभिसत्कृतः।।2.107.1।।
shlokas.translation: Wellhonoured in the midst of relatives, the celebrated Rama, elder brother of Lakshmana, then said to Bharata who was going to speak again.

shlokas.original:
उपपन्नमिदं वाक्यं यत्त्वमेवमभाषथाः।
जातः पुत्रो दशरथात्कैकेय्यां राजसत्तमात्।।2.107.2।।
shlokas.translation: Appropriate are the words spoken by you born to the celebrated king Dasaratha through Kaikeyi.

shlokas.original:
पुरा भ्रातः पिता न स्स मातरं ते समुद्वहन्।
मातामहे समाश्रौषीद्राज्यशुल्कमनुत्तमम्।।2.107.3।।
shlokas.translation: Earlier when our father was to wed your mother, O dear brother, he had promised to your maternal grandfather that he would confer (on her) a kingdom with good revenue.

shlokas.original:
दैवासुरे च सङ्ग्रामे जनन्यै तव पार्थिवः।
सम्प्रहृष्टो ददौ राजा वरमाराधितः प्रभुः।।2.107.4।।
shlokas.translation: Thereafter in the battle between gods and demons, the able king (Dasaratha) pleased  (with your mother) granted her a boon (as a token of gratitude).

shlokas.original:
ततस्सा सम्प्रतिश्राव्य तव माता यशस्विनी।
अयाचत नरश्रेष्ठं द्वौ वरौ वरवर्णिनी।।2.107.5।।
तव राज्यं नरव्याघ्र मम प्रव्राजनं तथा।
तौ च राजा तदा तस्यै नियुक्तः प्रददौ वरौ।।2.107.6।।
shlokas.translation: Thereafter, O tiger among men, your illustrious mother of fair complexion, under oath demanded of that best of men, king Dasaratha, the two boons, namely kingdom for you and banishment for me. Bound by such agreement (oath), the king granted her the boons.

shlokas.original:
ततः सा सम्प्रतिश्राव्य तव माता यशस्विनी।
अयाचत नरश्रेष्ठं द्वौ वरौ वरवर्णिनी।।2.107.5।।
तव राज्यं नरव्याघ्र मम प्रव्राजनं तथा।
तच्च राजा तदा तस्यै नियुक्तः प्रददौ वरौ।।2.107.6।।
shlokas.translation: Thereafter, O tiger among men, your illustrious mother of fair complexion, under oath demanded of that best of men, king Dasaratha, the two boons, namely kingdom for you and banishment for me. Bound by such agreement (oath), the king granted her the boons.

shlokas.original:
तेन पित्राऽहमप्यत्र नियुक्तः पुरुषर्षभ।
चतुर्दश वने वासं वर्षाणि वरदानिकम्।।2.107.7।।
shlokas.translation: O best of men, that is why I have been ordered by father to live in the forest for fourteen years as per the (condition of the) boon.

shlokas.original:
सोऽहं वनमिदं प्राप्तो निर्जनं लक्ष्मणान्वितः।
सीतया चाप्रतिद्वन्द्व स्सत्यवादे स्थितः पितुः।।2.107.8।।
shlokas.translation: I have come to this lonely forest accompanied by Lakshmana and Sita, bent upon upholding the truth of our unrivalled father's words.

shlokas.original:
भवानपि तथेत्येव पितरं सत्यवादिनम्।
कर्तुमर्हति राजेन्द्र क्षिप्रमेवाभिषेचनात्।।2.107.9।।
shlokas.translation: O Indra among kings, let your coronation be performed without delay since you are  worthy and at the same time the truthfulness of our father's words be honoured.

shlokas.original:
ऋणान्मोचय राजानं मत्कृते भरत प्रभुम्।
पितरं चापि धर्मज्ञं मातरं चाभिनन्दय।।2.107.10।।
shlokas.translation: O Bharata, release for my sake the competent king from his debt (of vow) and honour the righteous father and the mother as well.

shlokas.original:
श्रूयते धीमता तात श्रुतिर्गीता यशस्विना।
गयेन यजमानेन गयेष्वेव पितॄन् प्रति।।2.107.11।।
shlokas.translation: Dear (brother), it is heard that long ago an illustrious king while performing a sacrifice intended for ancestors at a sacred place known as Gaya, the sacrificer Gaya chanted a Vedic hymn as follows:

shlokas.original:
पुन्नाम्नो नरकाद् यस्मात् पितरं त्रायते सुतः।
तस्मात् पुत्र इति प्रोक्तः पितॄन् यः पाति सर्वतः।।2.107.12।।
shlokas.translation: Putra is so called as he delivers his father from a hell known as Put and protects the ancestors in all possible ways.

shlokas.original:
एष्टव्या बहवः पुत्रा गुणवन्तो बहुश्रुताः।
तेषां वै समवेतानामपि कश्चिद्गयां व्रजेत्।।2.107.13।।
shlokas.translation: One should desire many virtuous and learned sons so that at least one of them may go to Gaya (to perform the father's obsequies).

shlokas.original:
एवं राजर्षय स्सर्वे प्रतीता राजनन्दन।
तस्मात्राहि नरश्रेष्ठ पितरं नरकात्प्रभो।।2.107.14।।
shlokas.translation: O enhancer of the king's delight, O best of men rajarsis have decided this way. You may, therefore, O lord, protect your father against hell.

shlokas.original:
अयोध्यां गच्छ भरत प्रकृतीरनुरञ्जय।
शत्रुघ्नसहितो वीर सह सर्वैर्द्विजातिभिः।।2.107.15।।
shlokas.translation: O valiant Bharata, go to Ayodhya accompanied by Satrughna and the twiceborn (brahmins) and keep the subjects delighted like pleased (with your rule).

shlokas.original:
प्रवेक्ष्ये दण्डकारण्यमहमप्यविलम्बयन्।
आभ्यान्तु सहितो राजन् वैदेह्या लक्ष्मणेन च।।2.107.16।।
shlokas.translation: O king (Bharata) I shall also enter the Dandaka forest without delay in the company of only these two -- the princess from Videha (Sita) and Lakshmana.

shlokas.original:
त्वं राजा भरत भव स्वयं नराणां वन्यानामहमपि राजराण्मृगाणाम्।
गच्छ त्वं पुरवरमद्य सम्प्रहृष्टस्संहृष्टस्त्वहमपि दण्डकान्प्रवेक्ष्ये।।2.107.17।।
shlokas.translation: O Bharata, be the king of men. I shall also become the supreme king of the wild beasts in the forest. Go now happily to Ayodhya, the best of cities, and I shall enter Dandaka forest with delight.

shlokas.original:
छायां ते दिनकरभाः प्रबाधमानां वर्षत्रं भरत करोतु मूर्ध्नि शीताम्।
एतेषामहमपि काननद्रुमाणां छायां तामतिशयिनीं सुखी श्रयिष्ये।।2.107.18।।
shlokas.translation: Let an umbrella protect you against (scorching) sunshine and provide its cool shade over your head, O Bharata As for me, I shall happily seek the shade of these dense  woodland trees.

shlokas.original:
शत्रुघ्नः कुशलमतिस्तु ते सहायस्सौमित्रिर्मम विदितः प्रधानमित्रम्।
चत्वारस्तनयवरा वयं नरेन्द्रं सत्यस्थं भरत चराम मा विषीद।।2.107.19।।
shlokas.translation: O Bharata, intelligent Satrughna will be your support and son of Sumitra (Lakshmana) as is wellknown will remain my best friend. All four of us, worthy sons of Dasaratha, shall follow the path of truth of our father.  इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अयोध्याकाण्डे सप्तोत्तरशततमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the one hundredseventh sarga in Ayodhyakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
